---
title: Errors
description: Error classes exported by @justwant/env.
icon: CircleAlert
---

`@justwant/env` exports one error class. All errors extend the native `Error` class.

## `EnvironmentError`

Thrown when one or more environment variables fail validation during `createEnv()` or `defineEnv()`.

```ts
import { EnvironmentError } from "@justwant/env";
```

### Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `"EnvironmentError"` | Always `"EnvironmentError"`. |
| `issues` | `readonly EnvIssue[]` | Array of every validation failure, one entry per invalid variable. |
| `message` | `string` | Human-readable summary listing all issues (inherited from `Error`). |

#### `EnvIssue`

Each entry in `issues` is an `EnvIssue`:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `key` | `string` | The name of the environment variable that failed. |
| `message` | `string` | The validation message for that variable. |

### Example

```ts
import { createEnv, EnvironmentError } from "@justwant/env";
import * as v from "valibot";

try {
  const env = createEnv({
    schema: {
      PORT: v.pipe(v.string(), v.regex(/^\d+$/)),
    },
  });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof EnvironmentError) {
    console.error(`${err.issues.length} invalid variable(s)`);

    for (const issue of err.issues) {
      console.error(`  ${issue.key}: ${issue.message}`);
    }
  }
}
```
